text: In software development, testing plays a vital role in ensuring quality. Unit tests check individual components, like functions or methods, to confirm they work correctly in isolation. Integration tests, by contrast, verify that different parts of the system interact properly together. One popular approach is test-driven development, where you write tests before writing the actual code. This method encourages clear planning and helps catch errors early. Together, these practices build confidence. When a team has strong test coverage, they can make changes to the codebase with less fear of breaking existing features, leading to more reliable and maintainable software.
